item.title: Stolen Tuesday MEMES Dump #211 (For your viewing pleasure)
item.description:
2. When the quite friend speaks up
3
4. How I expect 2025 to be for the world
5. Cat playing the role of both the human and the cat
6. That's a neat trick
7. How it looks when someone "helps" you during college team assignments
8. You can either learn skills or to be skilled at video editing!
9. Dog is very angry!
10. A good boiii but not the smartest boiii
11
12
13
14. I'm sorry sir but I'm going to need to see some id, ego and super-ego
15. I like narrative games where you can mess around with The Narrator! This is the latest thing I added to the game. Kind of reminds of the Broom Closet from The Stanley Parable. If you like this style of games, please consider wishlisting my wacky comedic narrative game here: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_Or_Youll_Delete_The_Multiverse/

item.title: This pasta sauce is now 8% smaller and water is now first ingredient vs tomatoes
item.description: Bonus: 450mg of potassium is now 13% of DV! Since the ingredients are being changed that much, are we sure that the nutrition facts are still accurate?
item.dc:creator: psuedon

item.title: Homemade beef pot pie recipe!
item.description: https://www.triedandtruerecipe.com/beef-pot-pie/ I'm having so much fun learning how to bake -- I feel like it's opened up a whole new world of possibilities! This beef pot pie is so rich and comforting and perfect for a cold day! Let me know if you have any questions about the recipe; I am happy to answer them!!! I am really enjoying sharing my recipes here -- if there is anything you'd like to see more of or any recipe requests, let me know!! Pie crust: 1½ cups all-purpose flour ½ cup cake flour ½ teaspoon kosher salt ¾ cups unsalted butter (1½ sticks, chilled and small-diced) ¼ cup shortening (chilled) ½ cup cold water Beef pot pie filling: 1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil 2 pounds beef stew cubes ( or tenderloin tips) 2 cups diced vegetables (such as carrots, celery, and onion; see Note 1) 2 teaspoons kosher salt (plus more to taste) 6 cloves garlic (peeled and minced) 1 tablespoon fresh thyme (minced; or use 1 teaspoon dry thyme) 2 teaspoons fresh rosemary (minced; or use 1 teaspoon dry rosemary) 2 tablespoons tomato paste 3 tablespoons butter ⅓ cup all-purpose flour 1 cup red wine 2 cups beef broth 1 cup frozen peas Kosher salt and black pepper to taste Egg wash: 1 egg (beaten) 1 tablespoon whole milk Make the pie crust: Combine the all-purpose flour, cake flour, and kosher salt in a large bowl and use a fork to mix them. Add the cold, diced butter and the chilled shortening to the bowl of flour. Use a pastry cutter to blend the fat into the flour until a crumbly, coarse mixture forms. Mix the cold water into the flour until a dough ball forms. Don’t worry if there are a few loose crumbles. Use your hands to pull everything together into a dough ball. Dump the dough onto a large piece of plastic wrap and use your hands to gather any loose crumbs into the dough ball. Wrap the plastic wrap tightly around the ball of dough. Refrigerate for at least 1 hour or up to 2 days. Start the pot pie filling: While the dough chills, make the pot pie filling. Pat the beef cubes dry and season with 2 teaspoons kosher salt and 1 teaspoon black pepper. Heat 1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil in a wide pot over medium-high heat. Add half the beef cubes and cook for 3 to 5 minutes per side until browned all over. Transfer to a bowl and continue browning the rest of the beef cubes for 3 to 5 minutes per side until all the beef is browned. Adjust the heat as needed to prevent the beef from burning. Transfer them to the bowl of browned beef. Leave any fond in the bottom of the pot. If needed, add another small drizzle of oil to the pot and turn the heat to medium. Add the diced celery, carrots, and onions to the pot and cook for 5 minutes, turning regularly, until the vegetables soften. Add a very scant amount of salt. Add the garlic, thyme, and rosemary and stir to combine. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Toast the flour: Add 2 tablespoons tomato paste to the vegetables and stir to coat. Cook for 1 minute until the paste sizzles and deepens in color. Melt the butter into the vegetables. Once bubbly, add ⅓ cup all-purpose flour and stir to coat. Cook for 2 minutes until the flour becomes nutty and aromatic. Add the liquid: Add 1 cup of red wine and bring to a boil. Whisk until a slurry forms. Add the beef broth and continue whisking until completely smooth. Taste and adjust the seasonings. Be careful not to add too much salt; the stew will become saltier as it cooks. Simmer the pie filling: Add the browned beef and any juices from the bowl to the pot. Bring to a boil. Cover and reduce the heat to low. Simmer for 2 hours, stirring the stew occasionally to prevent it from sticking. Add a splash of water if the stew reduces too quickly. Taste and adjust the seasonings. Finish the pot pie filling: Add the frozen peas to the stew and stir to incorporate. Turn off the heat. Cool the pot pie filling: Transfer the pot pie filling to a wide dish. Cover loosely with cling wrap and let stand for at least 30 minutes to 1 hour. I recommend transferring it to a wider dish instead of a deep bowl for a more rapid cool-down. See Note 2. Roll out the pie crust: Preheat the oven to 425ºF. Mix the beaten egg and the milk in a bowl and set aside. Remove the chilled dough from the refrigerator. Cut the ball in half and return half of the dough to the refrigerator. Place the other half on a lightly floured surface and press it into a disc. Use a floured rolling pin to roll the dough into a circle about 1 inch larger than your pie dish. Carefully lift the dough from the counter and place it into a 9-inch pie dish. See Note 3. Add another sprinkle of flour to your work surface. Roll out the other half of the dough into a circle about 1 inch larger than the pie dish. Make the pot pie: Pour the cooled pot pie filling into the prepared pie dish. Carefully lift the top crust from your surface and place it on top. Trim off any excess and discard it or use it for decoration. Use your fingers to seal the pie crust edges. Crimp the crust with your fingers or a fork if you like. Use a sharp knife to create a few small slits in the top of the crust to allow the steam to escape. Brush the crust with the egg and milk mixture. You likely won’t use all of it. Bake the pot pie: Place the pie dish on a large baking sheet. Transfer to the oven for 35 to 40 minutes or until the crust is golden brown and flaky. Rotate the pie once midway through baking for even browning. Remove from the oven and let stand for 20 minutes. To serve: Cut the pie and transfer to plates. Serve and enjoy! Notes Note 1: I used 2 ribs of celery, 1 small yellow onion, and 2 small carrots to yield about 2 cups of vegetables. Try not to do more than 2 cups of diced vegetables as it will create too much filling for the pie. You can mix and match as you like. Try replacing a carrot with a parsnip, turnip, or potato instead. Note 2: The pot pie filling should be no warmer than 50ºF. After it stands for 30 minutes to 1 hour, you can either proceed with making the pot pie or refrigerate the filling for up to 1 day before making the pot pie. For food safety reasons, do not let the pie filling stand at room temperature for more than 2 hours. Note 3: Though unnecessary, you may wish to lightly grease the pie dish with a touch of shortening. I add a very small amount of shortening for extra security against the crust sticking to the dish.

item.title: Elon Musk Biographer Details Why He Thinks Billionaire Is ‘Going Mad’
item.description: The Harvard Law-educated biographer Seth Abramson speculated Monday that Musk might be “going mad” in a setting for all to see—brought on by his growing stress, history of mental illness, and self-described heavy drug use. “I legitimately believe Elon Musk may be going mad,” he posted to X. “I’m a Musk biographer who has been tracking his online behavior for the last two years—and given that he’s admitted to all of mental illness, heavy drug use, and crippling stress, it is now reasonable to fear he is deeply unwell.” https://www.thedailybeast.com/elon-musk-biographer-seth-abramson-details-why-he-thinks-billionaire-is-going-mad/
item.dc:creator: charondaboatman

item.dc:creator: SkyPigeon123

item.title: Voices for the Litterbox Animated Pilot! [OC]
item.description:
The search was long, but we finally found them! Introducing the voices of the Litterbox Family for the upcoming Animated Pilot!
FRAN will be played by the hilarious Esmarelda Villalobos, comedian, filmmaker and cat mom!
JOEL, by the masterful J Michael Tatum, known for his performances in anime such as Black Butler, Fairy Tail, Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood and Attack on Titan.
VINCENT to be played by the dynamic Brittney Karbowski, known for her work as Black Star in Soul Eater and Wendy Marvell in Fairy Tale, to name a few!
-And last but not least, little COOPER will be played by the adorable newcomer, Oliver Lucas!
The pilot is currently in development and we're hoping to release it to YouTube this year!! Follow LitterboxComics on your fav social media to keep up to date! :D
item.dc:creator: LitterboxComics

item.dc:creator: fortytwoembers

item.title: My Favorite women of Sci-Fi
item.description:
Everyone remembers Angelique Pettyjohn as Shahna in Star Trek: TOS S2:E16 The Gamesters of Triskelion. Her birth name was Dorothy Lee Perrins, and sometimes called Angel St. John, Heaven St. John, or Angelique. She was also in a series of B-movies like Hell's Belles and also in The Wizard of Speed and Time and a few other Sci-fi movies in her career.
Ayshea Brough - best known from the TV Series UFO. SHe was also a columnist for the magazine "Look-In" and worked as a motivational speaker under the name of Ayshea DeHavilland.
Cara Peters as Captain Mother in "Space Thing" from 1968, nicknaned Legs Benedict.
Many may recognize Caroline Munro from her role in The Spy Who Loved Me, but she was in many films from Starcrash to Doctor Who - The Monthly Adventures podcast to The Pocket Film of Superstitions releasted in 2023.
Born in Budapest, Hungary, Catherine Schell was in both Space:1999 as Maya and Servant of the Guardian, but she was also Countess Scarlioni in Doctor Who in the City of Death episodes with Tom Baker in 1979.
Patricia Barnstable and her sister Cyb Barnstable were in the TV series Quark. Cyb was also in two cheesy B-movies solo: So Fine and A Little Sex. All other appearances were with her sister as twins on The Love Boat, Major Effects and a few other appearances, but are most known for their TV commercials as one of the pairs of Doublemint Twins.
Kim Basinger in My Stepmother is an Alien and has been in numerous other movies from Batman to Cool World as Holli Wood, The Six Million Dollar Man and other credits throughour her career.
Markie Post in Buck Rogers in the 25th Century as Joella Cameron. She was also Electra Woman in Electra Woman and Dyna Girl, Transformers Prime as June Darby (voice only), and as Debra Dante in The Greatest American Hero. I still can't get over Christine Sullivan wearing that outfit though. :)
Nicola Bryant as Peri Brown in Doctor Who, and was in multiple episodes. The one I remember most is "Dimensions in Time" with Tom Baker, Peter Davidson, Colin Baker, and Sylvester McCoy along with well known companions Leela (Louise Jameson), and Ace (Sophie Aldred).
We all know Persis Khambatta from Star Trek The Motion Picture. She was also in Lois &Clark, MacGyver, Warrio of the Lost World, Megaforce, and some other interesting early credits to her name. Sadly, she passed at 50 years old in 1998 from a heart attack.
Raquel Welch in Fantastic Voyage. Need I say more?
Doctor Who companion Nyssa, played by Sarah Sutton. She is still active in various Doctor Who podcasts, with most of her career being around Dr. Who, but not entirely.
Louise Jameson as Leela, another Doctor Who Companion. She was also a survivor (uncredited) in Space:1999, the Omega Factor, and a long list of other works.
Lets not forget Erin Gray in Buck Rogers in the 25th Century. The one episode that sticks out is Season 2, Episode 10, "Shgoratchx" and the dwarves chanting "Off think, off think, off think!". :)
What is a good "Women of Sci-Fi" post without Wanda Ventham? Starred in the TV series UFO, and even in the Benedict Cumberbatch series Sherlock as Sherlock's mother and it fits because she is Benedict Comberbach's mother!
Yutte Stensgaard from Zeta One and Some Girls Do (as a robot). Although she is from the UK, her debut was in an Italian movie titled "The Girl with a Pistol".
Mary McDonnell as Larua Roslin in Battlestar Galactica (series from 2005). A remember her from the TV series E/R as Dr. Sheridan, and in the movie Independence Day.
And one more Doctor Who Companion, the greatest companion of all time to some, Sarah Jane Smith, played by Elisabeth Sladen. Her first appearance in Doctor Who was in 1973, and her last was posthumously in the TV movie Doctor Who: Exploration Earth in 2012.
item.dc:creator: JinxRocks
